amu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guerrero Amuzgo.
ate: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Atemble.
atom: noun. The smallest possible amount of matter which still retains its identity as a chemical element, now known to consist of a nucleus
automate: verb. To replace or enhance human labor with machines.
mae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bo-Rukul.
matte: noun. A decorative border around a picture used to inset and center the contents of a frame.
meat: noun. The flesh (muscle tissue) of a killed animal used as food.
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
moa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwan.
moat: noun. A deep, wide defensive ditch, normally filled with water, surrounding a fortified habitation.
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
mote: noun. A small particle; a speck.
motet: noun. A composition adapted to sacred words in the elaborate polyphonic church style; an anthem.
mott: noun. A copse or small grove of trees, especially live oak or elm.
motte: noun. A raised earth mound, often topped with a wooden or stone structure and surrounded with a ditch.
moue: noun. A pout, especially as expressing mock-annoyance or flirtatiousness.
mut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Muria.
mute: adjective. Not having the power of speech; dumb.
mutt: noun. A mongrel dog (or sometimes cat); an animal of mixed breed or uncertain origin.
oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
tam: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tamil.
tat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tatar.
tate: noun. Of video games, a vertical mode.
tau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Upper Tanana.
taut: adjective. Under tension, like a stretched bowstring, rope, or sail; tight.
tea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Temiar.
team: noun. A set of draught animals, such as two horses in front of a carriage.
teat: noun. The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female therian mammals, milk is secreted.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
toea: noun. A unit of currency, equivalent to one hundredth of a Papua New Guinean kina.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
tome: noun. One in a series of volumes.
tut: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Altaic languages.
uta: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Otank.
